_comment: End-to-end feature/physics-parity test, run against BOTH the original Flash projector (tools/pr2driver.py) and the OpenFL HTML5 port (tools/openfl_driver.py) from this one file. Identical inputs for both clients (no per-client branching). Flow: preloader/intro skip -> open Login -> clear any saved session via the '-' button so both clients land on the same name/pass form -> type haxe-port-test/haxe-port-test -> Log In -> favorites (heart) tab -> 'Don't Move JV' -> Play -> idle while the level's blocks push the character through the course on their own -> Race Complete! popup. The '-' (delete saved user) + confirm-OK clicks are no-ops on the fresh name/pass form, so the sequence works whether or not a session is saved. Times are seconds from app start (Flash window-ready after launch / OpenFL data-pr2-app-ready). 'launch'/'quit' are no-ops on the OpenFL driver. The port must be served through tools/dev_proxy.py (apiHost=/api) so the server list and login reach pr2hub.com. Assert the final shot with tools/check_race_complete.py.

steps._note: skip Jiggmin intro anim if present (harmless empty click otherwise)
'-' delete saved user (drops a remembered session so both clients show the name/pass form); no-op on the fresh form
confirm OK on the delete-account dialog; harmless stray click on empty dialog area when no confirm appeared

steps.name: levelSlot
steps._note: OpenFL waits for the favorites response and clicks the first rendered slot; Flash uses the stage-coordinate fallback

steps.name: play_bt
steps._note: OpenFL clicks as soon as the live Play button exists; Flash uses the stage-coordinate fallback
Race Complete! popup should be visible; includes timing headroom for slower machines
